summary: Decompiled the ftCo_DamageBind portion of ft_0C31 into a new ftCommon source/header pair, moving 600 bytes of fighter damage-bind logic out of asm and reporting 96.37% file match. The PR added C implementations/prototypes for ftCo_800C44CC, ftCo_800C4550, and the DamageBind action callbacks, expanded ftCommonData fields around offsets 0x658-0x674 needed by the formulas, moved local sdata2 literals into C, updated includes, and added the new object to obj_files.mk. There were no review comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Added new source file src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageBind.c with C for ftCo_800C44CC, ftCo_800C4550, ftCo_DamageBind_Anim, ftCo_DamageBind_IASA, ftCo_DamageBind_Phys, and ftCo_DamageBind_Coll.
Removed the corresponding ftCo_DamageBind assembly block from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s, leaving later ft_0C31 symbols such as ftCo_800C4724 in asm.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageBind.h and moved the DamageBind prototypes out of src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.h.
Added ftCo_DamageBind.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before the remaining ft_0C31.s.o entry.
Included ftCo_DamageBind.h from ftCo_Damage.c and ftCo_Init.c so the new prototypes are visible where common fighter damage/init code references them.
Expanded struct ftCommonData in src/melee/ft/types.h from a byte blob at +0x658 into named offset fields x658, x65C, x660, x664, x668, x66C, x670, x674, followed by UNK_T placeholders through x6D4.
Moved sdata2 literals ftCo_804D8D38, ftCo_804D8D40, and ftCo_804D8D44 into C as S32_TO_F32, 0.0f, and 1.0f equivalents.
ftCo_800C44CC now expresses the damage-bind eligibility check in C: requires fp->dmg.x1860 == 12, x2228_b2 clear, and excludes x2070.x2071_b0_3 values 5, 9, 11, and 12 before calling ftCo_800C4550.

ftCo_800C4550 now documents the high-level flow: clear/setup common damage state, branch to ftCo_80090780 when airborne, otherwise change to ftCo_MS_DamageBind and compute a duration/parameter using percent, handicap, Player_80033BB8, and ftCommonData fields.

DamageBind callbacks were reduced to simple behavior where possible: IASA is empty, Phys calls ft_80084F3C, Coll calls ft_80084280, and Anim decrements fp->x1A4C, calls ftCommon_8007DC08, then exits through ft_8008A2BC when the timer reaches zero.
smart_moves: Split only the matched DamageBind range into its own ftCo_DamageBind.c while keeping the rest of ft_0C31.s intact, minimizing risk and preserving object order via obj_files.mk.
Used the existing ftCommon action-state naming pattern, giving the new file/header semantic ownership instead of leaving these symbols in the broad ft_0C31 bucket.
Preserved address comments in the new header, which keeps cross-reference/search workflows intact during incremental decomp.
Expanded ftCommonData just enough for the offsets used by this action, keeping unknown trailing fields as UNK_T placeholders rather than over-naming unsupported data.
Kept literal labels explicitly declared in C, including the S32_TO_F32 double used by integer-to-float conversion, which is important for matching PPC floating conversion code.

Where high-level C was not fully matching, kept guarded inline assembly for larger functions, allowing readable reconstruction work while retaining an exact fallback path under matching-related defines.
Converted trivial callbacks directly to compact C wrappers/empty function, removing assembly where the control flow was unambiguous.
decomp_lessons: For action-state slices, migrate the callback family together: gate/helper function, action entry function, Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll. This makes header ownership and include updates straightforward.
When a function reads a sequence of common-data floats, expanding the struct by exact offsets can unlock readable formulas without needing final semantic names for every field.
Do not leave newly decompiled action-state prototypes in broad numbered headers if a semantic ftCo_* header exists or can be introduced.
Small wrapper callbacks are good first targets in an asm file because they remove assembly with low behavioral ambiguity and improve file score quickly.
PPC int-to-float sequences using xoris/lis 0x4330/lfd often require an S32_TO_F32 double literal; moving that literal carefully into C helps preserve matching.
Switch statements can capture compare/branch exclusion tables cleanly when the original assembly checks sparse action/substate values.

For partially matching large functions, it is acceptable in this codebase to pair a readable C version with guarded inline assembly, but future work should revisit the non-100% function rather than treating it as fully solved.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action files use the ftCo_<Action>.c and ftCo_<Action>.h pattern, here ftCo_DamageBind.
Action callbacks use suffixes _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll, as in ftCo_DamageBind_Anim and ftCo_DamageBind_Coll.
Unknown non-callback helpers remain address-based until semantics are proven, as with ftCo_800C44CC and ftCo_800C4550.
New prototypes include address comments such as /* 0C44CC */ before the declaration.
The new header guard follows the address-style GALE01_0C44CC pattern rather than a semantic file-name guard.
ftCommonData fields were named by offset, such as x658, x65C, x670, and x674, because no semantic names were evidenced in the PR.
Fighter fields used existing offset/bitfield names such as fp->dmg.x1860, fp->dmg.x1830_percent, fp->x2228_b2, and fp->x2070.x2071_b0_3.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted 182 lines of assembly from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s and replaced them with a new C object inserted immediately before the remaining ft_0C31.s object in TEXT_O_FILES.
Used explicit float/double literals ftCo_804D8D38, ftCo_804D8D40, and ftCo_804D8D44 in C to replace sdata2 entries formerly in the asm file.
Used ASM function definitions with conditional high-level C versus inline assembly blocks for ftCo_800C4550 and ftCo_DamageBind_Anim, preserving original instruction bodies under guarded paths.
Retained nofralloc inline assembly and #pragma peephole on in guarded fallback blocks for functions that were not fully matched by plain C in the slice.
Matched the trivial blr-only IASA function with an empty C function.
Matched Phys and Coll as one-call wrappers around ft_80084F3C and ft_80084280.
Reported scores show all functions except ftCo_800C4550 at 100%; ftCo_800C4550 remained 92.32%, driving the file score of 96.37%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftCo_800C44CC or ftCo_800C4550 to semantic DamageBind helper names.
Search for later expansion or semantic naming of ftCommonData fields x658 through x674.
Check subsequent work on ft_0C31.s around ftCo_800C4724 to see whether the object split/order pattern was reused.
Look for later PRs that remove the guarded inline assembly fallback from ftCo_800C4550 or improve its 92.32% match.
Search for other DamageBind references in ftCo_Damage.c and ftCo_Init.c to understand how ftCo_MS_DamageBind is wired into the common action table.
